skill.name: tavily-search
skill.description: Search the web through the Tavily CLI with LLM-oriented snippets, relevance scores, metadata, domain filters, time ranges, and search depth options.
skill.summary: Run Tavily web searches and return structured, LLM-ready source results.
skill.author: tavily-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified a high-risk remote installer pattern: the skill tells agents to run a curl-fetched script through bash before use. Most other detections are false positives from Markdown inline code, option tables, and relative documentation links, but the installer and external network CLI use make this unsafe to publish without changes.

high_findings.title: Remote Install Script Piped To Shell
high_findings.description: Line 17 instructs agents to run curl against an external installer URL and pipe the result directly into bash, followed by tvly login. This is a true positive because it executes unaudited remote code during skill use and creates supply-chain and credential exposure risk. No evidence found that the command is malicious, so this is high risk rather than a confirmed block.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact curl-to-bash pattern is present on line 17 and is semantically an installer command. The risk is confirmed, although malicious intent is not proven from the file contents.

high_findings.title: Mandatory Installation And Login Flow
high_findings.description: Lines 14 through 20 tell the agent to install tvly if missing and not skip the step. Combined with tvly login, this can push users toward executing installer code and starting authentication from a skill instruction rather than a trusted setup flow.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and adjacent to the installer command. The security impact depends on runtime tool enforcement and user approval, but the unsafe workflow is clear.

medium_findings.title: External Search CLI Sends Queries To Network Service
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to call tvly search commands that send user queries to Tavily and return web results. This is expected behavior for a search skill, but users should know that search terms and included domains may leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples directly invoke a web search CLI. The network behavior is inherent to the tool, but the file does not show hidden exfiltration beyond intended searches.

medium_findings.title: CLI Supports Writing Search Output To Files
medium_findings.description: Line 67 documents an output option that can save results to a file. This is a legitimate feature, but it adds filesystem impact if sensitive search results or raw page content are written to an uncontrolled path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The option is documented in the skill and likely legitimate. The risk is moderate because no unsafe path construction is shown.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command detections point to inline option names, command examples, and Markdown tables rather than Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are false positives unless copied and run by the agent as intended commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fenced code or inline code spans. They are not Ruby backtick execution syntax in an executable source file.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Misclassified As Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The ../ sequences on lines 22, 28, 90, and 91 are Markdown links to sibling skill documentation. No file read, write, or traversal operation is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is plain Markdown links, not executable filesystem access. This makes the static path traversal finding a strong false positive.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Not Supported By Context
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography detections at lines 3, 51, and 65 do not correspond to cryptographic code. No hash, cipher, signature, or password handling logic is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose and option-table content on these lines. There is no cryptographic implementation to assess.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl Installer Output Executed By bash
dangerous_patterns.description: The command on line 17 downloads an installer from https://cli.tavily.com/install.sh and executes it immediately with bash. This pattern prevents review of the downloaded script before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pipe-to-shell pattern is explicit and complete on one line. This is a well-known dangerous installation pattern.

content.value_statement: AI agents need current web sources when built-in knowledge is stale. This skill runs Tavily searches and returns structured results with snippets, scores, metadata, and optional filters.
actual_capabilities: Runs Tavily CLI web searches for user supplied queries.
Returns JSON-formatted results with snippets, relevance scores, and metadata.
Supports search depth choices from ultra-fast to advanced.
Filters results by included or excluded domains.
Limits results by time range, start date, end date, topic, and country.
Can request raw page content, image results, or AI answers when supported by the CLI.
limitations: Requires the Tavily CLI to be installed and authenticated before use.
Search requests are sent to an external Tavily service.
Result quality depends on Tavily indexing and the chosen search depth.
Raw content and image options may increase response size and processing time.

use_cases.title: Current Source Discovery
use_cases.description: Find recent articles, official pages, and relevant sources before writing an answer.

use_cases.title: Domain Focused Fact Checks
use_cases.description: Search trusted domains such as government, company, or publication sites for focused evidence.

use_cases.title: Development Research
use_cases.description: Find tutorials, documentation, or technical posts with optional raw content for deeper review.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Web Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for recent information about [topic]. Return the most relevant sources with short summaries.

prompt_templates.title: News Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Find recent news within a time window.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for news about [topic] from the past week. Prioritize reliable publishers and include publication context.

prompt_templates.title: Trusted Domain Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [topic] only on [domains]. Compare the strongest results and note any gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Source Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use deeper search settings and raw content for source evaluation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run an advanced search for [topic], include raw content for top sources, and identify the most useful evidence.

output_examples.input: Search for recent AI regulation updates in the United States.
output_examples.output: A ranked list of relevant sources with titles, URLs, snippets, dates when available, and notes on why each source matters.

output_examples.input: Find SEC guidance on climate disclosures from official domains.
output_examples.output: Focused results from trusted domains, including official page matches, short summaries, and relevance notes.

output_examples.input: Look up React hooks tutorials with raw content for the top pages.
output_examples.output: Top tutorial sources with extracted page text summaries, metadata, and suggestions for which source to inspect first.

best_practices: Use concise search queries under 400 characters.
Use domain filters when source trust matters.
Use time ranges for fast-moving topics and recent events.
anti_patterns: Do not send private, secret, or regulated data as search queries.
Do not rely on search snippets alone for high-stakes decisions.
Do not run remote installer commands without reviewing the source and approval path.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It uses the Tavily CLI to search the web and return structured results for AI research workflows.

faq.question: Does it require an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. Searches are sent to Tavily, and the CLI must be installed and authenticated.

faq.question: Can it search only trusted websites?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents include-domain and exclude-domain options for narrowing search results.

faq.question: Can it find recent news?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports news topic searches and time ranges such as day, week, month, and year.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: The skill tells agents to pipe a remote installer script directly into bash, which can execute unreviewed code.

faq.question: Is malicious intent confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The audit found a dangerous installation pattern, but no evidence of confirmed malware in the reviewed file.
